title: Backup, Point-in-Time Restore, Rebuild, and Recovery Ordering · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A disability-led research network incrementally builds rehearsed TypeScript service recovery system from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and browser fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, HTTP-client, Express, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, async-cleanup, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of rehearsed TypeScript service recovery system while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, expli…
objectives: Assign RPO, RTO, encryption, retention, restore, and rebuild strategy to accounts, subscriptions, reader state, feeds, revisions, raw evidence, and telemetry.
Verify backup schedule, WAL continuity, age, checksum, encryption, access, capacity, catalog, and application compatibility.
Restore into isolation, block outbound fetch, validate schema and app versions, run invariants, reconcile counts, and review tenant and reader data.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Recovery Data Classes
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 4761 during a clean emitted-package and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove data-class objectives, backup eligibility, isolated restore, safe resume sequence, and drills; every Promise, AbortSignal listener, timer, stream, pool client, transaction, lease, and handle needs an owner; then change one PostgreSQL constraint, transaction, migration, upsert, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Assign RPO, RTO, encryption, retention, restore, and rebuild strategy to accounts, subscriptions, reader state, feeds, revisions, raw evidence, and telemetry.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Recovery Data Classes.

For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Recovery Data Classes, keep Feed Ts Recovery Data Classes connected to executable TypeScript unknown-validation, async, PostgreSQL, Express, reader, package, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
